title: Year 4 New Zealand Curriculum - Ministry of Education
content:
## Year 4 New Zealand Curriculum
Information to help you understand what your child will have learned by the end of their fourth year at school, and ideas or activities to help support their learning at home.
- Guide for the New Zealand Curriculum – Years 0 to 8
- First 6 months at school
- Year 1 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 2 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 3 New Zealand Curriculum
- Year 4 New Zealand Curriculum English in Year 4 Reading books at home in Year 4 Mathematics and Statistics in Year 4 Health and Physical Education in Year 4 Science in Year 4 Technology in Year 4 Social Sciences in Year 4 The Arts in Year 4
- Reading books at home in Year 4
- Mathematics and Statistics in Year 4

We’re inviting parents and caregivers to share their feedback on the Parent Portal to help us improve the information and support we provide. Take a few minutes to complete our short survey.
Parent Portal survey – Survey Monkey

## By the end of Year 4 #
Knowing what your child is learning will help you support and guide them as they begin learning reading, writing and maths every day.
- English in Year 4 For English in Year 4, your child’s teacher will focus on oral language, reading and writing. Find out more
For English in Year 4, your child’s teacher will focus on oral language, reading and writing.
- Reading books at home in Year 4 Find out about supporting your child’s reading in the fourth year of school. Find out more

### Reading books at home in Year 4
Find out about supporting your child’s reading in the fourth year of school.
- Mathematics and Statistics in Year 4 In Years 4, 5 and 6 (phase 2), the focus at school is on helping children expand their knowledge and work together with others. Find out more

### Mathematics and Statistics in Year 4
In Years 4, 5 and 6 (phase 2), the focus at school is on helping children expand their knowledge and work together with others.
- Health and Physical Education in Year 4 In Year 4, your child will learn about different bodies, respectful relationships, digital safety, and movement skills in games and sports. Find out more

### Health and Physical Education in Year 4
In Year 4, your child will learn about different bodies, respectful relationships, digital safety, and movement skills in games and sports.
- Science in Year 4 In Year 4, your child will begin to investigate, explain, and observe using basic scientific methods and ways of thinking and communicating. Find out more
In Year 4, your child will begin to investigate, explain, and observe using basic scientific methods and ways of thinking and communicating.
- Technology in Year 4 In Year 4, your child creates and tests designs, making improvements, developing their understanding of materials and ingredients, and collecting feedback to inform designs. Find out more

### Technology in Year 4
In Year 4, your child creates and tests designs, making improvements, developing their understanding of materials and ingredients, and collecting feedback to inform designs.
- Social Sciences in Year 4 In Year 4, your child will start to connect important concepts such as migration and democracy, learning about Te Tiriti o Waitangi, civic participation, and population change. Find out more

### Social Sciences in Year 4
In Year 4, your child will start to connect important concepts such as migration and democracy, learning about Te Tiriti o Waitangi, civic participation, and population change.
- The Arts in Year 4 In Year 4, your child will build confidence while making thoughtful artistic choices, applying creative techniques, and exploring cultural and historical influences on art forms. Find out more
In Year 4, your child will build confidence while making thoughtful artistic choices, applying creative techniques, and exploring cultural and historical influences on art forms.
